title: trifolium rusbyi subsp. oreganum
intro: Trifolium rusbyi subsp. oreganum, also known as Rusby's clover, is a subspecies of perennial plant in the pea family that is native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria and fungi. The health benefits of Trifolium rusbyi subsp. oreganum include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. Traditionally, the plant has been used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves, which are said to promote skin health and soothe minor injuries.
